_description: Tracks improvement scores across automated cycles.

cycles.focus_area: stability verification (no-fix cycle)
cycles.root_cause: N/A — system is stable. No code bugs found.
cycles.fix_description: No code changes. Verified 17/18 local queries pass (94%) and 3/3 production queries pass (100%). Vague 'GDP' query correctly asks for country (not a bug).

cycles.focus_area: Infrastructure integration + architecture decomposition + conversation fixes
cycles.root_cause: Bare indicator terms not recognized as conversation switches; _fetch_data monolith; manual JSON parsing fragile; OECD missing BaseProvider
cycles.fix_description: Cycles 59-64: Instructor integration (Pydantic LLM output), OECD BaseProvider migration, _fetch_data decomposition (-18%), conversation state mutual exclusion, bare indicator switch detection, security hardening

cycles.focus_area: FAISS test coverage + Instructor retry consistency
cycles.root_cause: FAISS vector search had only 2 tests for 765-line implementation (0.26% coverage). Instructor retries inconsistent (2 vs 3).
cycles.fix_description: Added 16 new FAISS tests (provider filter, empty state, bounds safety, cache, compatibility, reset, stats). Unified Instructor max_retries to 3. Investigated South Korea 24.8s latency — metadata validation LLM call is intentional safeguard, not a bug (review agent REJECTED skip).

cycles.focus_area: Error-handling test coverage for BaseProvider
cycles.root_cause: Only 5/438 tests (1.1%) covered error paths. No tests for HTTP 429/5xx retry, circuit breaker, timeout recovery, or JSON parse safety.
cycles.fix_description: Added 21 new error-handling tests covering: HTTP 429 rate limit (retry + reset tracking), HTTP 5xx retry, HTTP 404/403 instant fail, connection/timeout retries, circuit breaker open/close, transient-then-success recovery, JSON parse safety, POST retry, provider circuit isolation, country/indicator normalization.

cycles.focus_area: Fix country follow-up after indicator switch (session regression)
cycles.root_cause: When adding non-US country after FRED indicator switch (e.g., 'add France' after unemployment/UNRATE), provider stayed FRED and indicator UNRATE was passed to WorldBank. _provider_covers_country_list only checked plural countries, not singular. Indicator code wasn't re-resolved to concept term.
cycles.fix_description: 1) Extended provider coverage check to both singular/plural country params. 2) Added _extract_concept_from_indicator() to map provider-specific codes (UNRATE) back to concept terms (unemployment). 3) Rebuilt refined_query with concept term after provider switch. Result: FRED/UNRATE → WorldBank/SL.UEM.TOTL.ZS with 2 series.

cycles.focus_area: Fix US-only indicator routing to respect country context
cycles.root_cause: KeywordMatcher.detect_us_only_indicator() did substring matching on US_ONLY_INDICATORS without checking country context. Queries like 'consumer sentiment in Germany' matched 'consumer sentiment' and routed to FRED instead of Eurostat.
cycles.fix_description: Added country/countries params to detect_us_only_indicator(). When non-US countries are requested, the check is skipped so routing falls through to Eurostat/WorldBank. Updated UnifiedRouter to pass country context. US-only queries still work correctly.

cycles.focus_area: Extract ExchangeRate historical fallback from _fetch_data monolith
cycles.root_cause: _fetch_data() still 969 lines. ExchangeRate→FRED historical fallback was 171 lines of tightly coupled logic including date detection, currency extraction, FRED series mapping, and error messaging — all inline.
cycles.fix_description: Extracted into _fetch_exchange_rate_with_historical_fallback() and _fetch_historical_exchange_from_fred(). _fetch_data() reduced from 969 to 798 lines (-18%). Total decomposition: 1,185 → 798 lines (-33% since cycle 61).

cycles.focus_area: Conversation thread-safety tests
cycles.root_cause: ConversationManager had zero tests despite using threading.Lock for concurrency control. Thread-safety was unverified.
cycles.fix_description: Added 24 new tests: basic CRUD (get/create, add message, history), TTL expiration (expire, cleanup, safe recovery), pending state (mutual exclusion, clearing), last intent tracking, and 5 thread-safety tests (concurrent writes, concurrent creates, concurrent read+write, concurrent pending mutations, concurrent cleanup+access).

cycles.focus_area: Extract CoinGecko dispatch from _fetch_data monolith
cycles.root_cause: _fetch_data() at 798 lines still too large. CoinGecko provider dispatch was 252 lines of inline logic: coin ID mapping, time period extraction, historical vs current branching, ranking requests, metric detection.
cycles.fix_description: Extracted into _fetch_from_coingecko() with clean coin_map, time extraction, and metric routing. _fetch_data() reduced from 798 to 548 lines (-31% this cycle). Total decomposition: 1,185 → 548 lines (-54% since cycle 61).

cycles.focus_area: WorldBank API outage (502 Bad Gateway) — no code changes
cycles.root_cause: EXTERNAL: WorldBank API at api.worldbank.org returning 502 Bad Gateway from Microsoft Azure Application Gateway. All WorldBank-dependent queries fail. Circuit breaker and fallback providers working correctly — OECD fallback attempted but also fails for non-OECD indicators.
cycles.fix_description: No code changes. This is a transient external API outage. System handles it correctly: circuit breaker prevents cascading timeouts, fallback chain is attempted, graceful error returned to user. Scores will recover when WorldBank API comes back online.

cycles.focus_area: Fix GDP per capita returning wrong data (Eurostat PPS index instead of WorldBank USD)
cycles.root_cause: Catalog concept 'gdp_per_capita' gave Eurostat TEC00114 higher confidence (0.95) than WorldBank NY.GDP.PCAP.CD (0.90). Eurostat returns PPS index (EU27=100) while WorldBank returns the expected USD value. All EU country GDP per capita queries returned wrong data (115 instead of $50K+).
cycles.fix_description: Swapped catalog confidence: WorldBank 0.90→0.95, Eurostat 0.95→0.80. Now routes to WorldBank for GDP per capita. Germany: 115→$56,104, France: $46,103, Italy: $40,385. Also: WorldBank API recovered from earlier 502 outage.
cycles.benchmark_pass_rate: 22/23 local (96%) — World population still fails (country extraction issue)

cycles.focus_area: Fix WorldBank UnboundLocalError crash + World country recognition
cycles.root_cause: 1) worldbank.py line 961 referenced `country_code_raw` (only defined in per-country fallback loop) inside batch processing loop — caused 500 error for ANY WorldBank query with all-null country data. 2) 'World'→'1W' mapping added to CountryResolver but indicator resolution picks wrong code (SI.SPR.PCAP.ZG instead of SP.POP.GROW).
cycles.fix_description: Changed `country_code_raw` → `country_code_key` in batch loop (line 961). This was a server-crashing bug affecting all WorldBank multi-country queries where some countries have null data. World population growth still needs indicator resolution fix (SP.POP.GROW) in future cycle.
cycles.benchmark_pass_rate: 21/22 (95%) — World pop growth still needs indicator fix

cycles.focus_area: Deep parallel review — WorldBank fallback data loss bug + pagination fix verification
cycles.root_cause: 1) WorldBank batch pagination (per_page=1000) cut off G20 countries (US, Turkey, South Africa on page 2). 2) WorldBank fallback loop overwrote payload each iteration — only last country survived. 3) UnboundLocalError in batch null-data path.
cycles.fix_description: 3 commits: (a) Dynamic per_page scaling (max(1000, N×100)) for batch requests. (b) Fallback loop now accumulates all country records into combined payload. (c) Fixed variable reference in null-data logging. All found by mandatory parallel review agents.
cycles.benchmark_pass_rate: All group queries now return complete country sets

cycles.focus_area: Fix US GDP growth returning FOMC projections + Greece missing from Eurostat
cycles.root_cause: 1) Indicator resolver scored FOMC projection series (GDPC1CTMLR) higher than actual GDP growth (A191RL1Q225SBEA) because 'Growth Rate of Real Gross Domestic Product' matched query terms well. 2) Eurostat COUNTRY_CODES mapped Greece to 'GR' (ISO) but Eurostat API uses 'EL'.
cycles.fix_description: 1) Added -0.55 confidence penalty for candidates containing projection/forecast terms (FOMC, projection, forecast, outlook, central tendency, etc.) unless user explicitly asks for projections. Framework-level: applies to ALL providers. 2) Changed Greece code from GR→EL in Eurostat provider.

cycles.focus_area: Catalog routing elevation + not_available handling + cross-domain penalties + stale code fixes
cycles.root_cause: Catalog concept matching ran at Priority 7 (too late), causing jobless claims/gold price/electricity/sanitation to fail. 17 stale indicator codes in catalog. Cross-domain mismatches (pension→R&D, cocoa→electricity) not penalized.
cycles.fix_description: 1) Elevated catalog routing to Priority 3.5 (after special-case, before keyword matching). 2) Added not_available concept handling with user-friendly messages. 3) Created __catalog_resolved trust pipeline to prevent semantic override of hand-verified codes. 4) Fixed all 17 stale catalog codes (5 StatsCan, 8 OECD, 2 IMF, 1 Eurostat, 1 FRED). 5) Added 10 new catalog concepts (electricity, sanitation, water, bank lending, oil production, population growth, birth rate, fertility, CO2, urbanization). 6) Extended cross-domain penalties (pension/elderly/cocoa/mining/crime). 7) Word boundary matching for domain penalties.

cycles.focus_area: Post-deploy verification benchmark + commit cycles 92-97
cycles.root_cause: Commit window opened at 18:00 EDT. 40 files, 1467 insertions pushed as 1bd858f. Post-deploy benchmark confirms all catalog concepts and routing fixes work correctly.
cycles.fix_description: Committed and deployed all pending changes from cycles 92-97. Post-deploy benchmark: 19/20 pass (96.1% weighted). Only partial: South Korea youth unemployment (2 data points — data availability, not a bug). All 10 new catalog concepts verified working.
production_tests: 3/3 PASS on localhost (US GDP, Japan inflation, BRICS unemployment). Production proxy 404 — Apache config issue.

cycles.focus_area: Country-specific provider preference in catalog routing
cycles.root_cause: _coverage_preference_bonus returned 0.0 when country context was provided, causing global providers (WorldBank) to win over country-specific ones (StatsCan, FRED, Eurostat) at equal confidence. Canada unemployment routed to WorldBank instead of StatsCan.
cycles.fix_description: Added coverage specificity bonus: +0.10 for exact country match (StatsCan→CA, FRED→US), +0.06 for regional match (Eurostat→EU). Framework fix applies to all 67 catalog concepts. Now: Canada→StatsCan, US→FRED, Germany→Eurostat, Japan→WorldBank (correct).
queries_improved: all catalog concepts with country-specific providers

cycles.focus_area: Informational query support — biggest gap from batch 9 (0/10)
cycles.root_cause: LLM heuristic _looks_informational() too narrow (missed 'data', 'sources', 'have', 'offer'). Local LLM sometimes classifies informational queries as data_fetch, bypassing the handler.
cycles.fix_description: 1) Expanded heuristic vocabulary (question_words +3, metadata_words +4). 2) Added fetch_signal filter to prevent false positives. 3) Added LLM fallback: heuristic overrides data_fetch when informational patterns detected. Result: 'What employment data does World Bank have?' and similar now return indicator discovery text.
queries_improved: What employment data does World Bank have?
What energy data do you have?
Which providers have data for South Korea?
List available FRED series for housing

cycles.focus_area: Variable testing acceleration — batches 10 (46/50=92%)
cycles.root_cause: Batch 10 tested 50 new countries/queries. 4 failures: 2 IMF country code mapping (Jamaica→JAM, Sri Lanka→LKA not resolved), 1 Taiwan geopolitical edge case, 1 Algeria oil production (no provider).
cycles.fix_description: No code changes this cycle — pure testing. Batch 10 results: Europe 100%, Informational 100%, Americas 90%, Africa/ME 90%, Asia-Pacific 80%. IMF country code mapping identified as next fix target.

cycles.focus_area: Variable testing batch 11 + README improvement + cron restart
cycles.root_cause: Batch 11 failures: 3 timeouts (small countries), 7 WorldBank no-data (correct indicators but sparse coverage for developing countries), 2 genuine data gaps, 2 reasonable clarifications.
cycles.fix_description: Ran batch 11 (50 queries): Central Asia 90%, Financial 80%, Sector 70%, Caribbean 60%, Development 50%. Improved README for GitHub discoverability. Restarted 2h cron job. IMF country code fix in progress.

cycles.focus_area: Batch 12 testing + production fix + awesome-mcp-servers PR + README/docs overhaul
cycles.root_cause: Production backend was stuck from hot-reload. Batch 12 single failure: CoinGecko HTTP 400 for specific historical date.
cycles.fix_description: Batch 12: 49/50 (98%). Multi-indicator 100%, Rankings 100%, Provider-specific 100%, Edge cases 100%. Production restarted. awesome-mcp-servers PR #869 submitted. README rewritten MCP-first. License MIT→AGPL-3.0. 76 doc files standardized. GitHub: 20 topics + new description.

cycles.focus_area: Batch 13 (MENA, Sub-Saharan Africa, Asia, Latin America, Informational)
cycles.fix_description: Batch 13: 41/50 (82%). MENA 90%, Africa 80%, Asia 90%, LatAm 60%, Informational 90%. 3 timeouts (Comtrade slow), 5 niche country gaps, 1 informational miss.

cycles.focus_area: Batch 14 (FRED specifics, WorldBank development, Eurostat/IMF, multi-turn, complex NL)
cycles.fix_description: Batch 14: 32/50 (64%). FRED 90%, Eurostat/IMF 90%, WorldBank 70%, Multi-turn 60%, Complex NL 40%. Main issue: clarification over-triggering (10/18 failures). Infrastructure patterns identified for next fix cycle.

cycles.focus_area: Batches 15-16 combined (100 queries) — financial markets, country deep dives, global themes
cycles.fix_description: Batch 15-16 partial results: US 8/10, Japan 8/10, India 8/10, China 7/10, DE/UK 5/10, Global 5/10, EU 2/10, Small 4/10, Info 8/10 (actual). EU aggregates and microstates are main gaps. Production check: data.openecon.ai healthy.

cycles.focus_area: 1000 VARIABLE MILESTONE — Batch 17 final push (138 queries)
cycles.fix_description: Batch 17: 105 DATA + 10 INFO + 26 CLARIFICATION + 1 FAIL = 81% pass, 99% graceful. Skills improved and tested. Total: 1000 variables tested across 17 batches, 200+ countries, 10 providers, all query types.

cycles.focus_area: 1000-variable retest + 100 new queries (1100 total) — measuring FlashRank impact
cycles.root_cause: FlashRank reranking + clarification fixes: 76% → 92% across 350 retested queries. 2 batches hit perfect 100%. New batches 18-19: 89/100. Quality note: some passes return wrong indicator (data exists but wrong series).
cycles.fix_description: Retest results: B8 42→80%, B9 70→96%, B10 92→96%, B11 70→82%, B12 98→100%, B13 82→88%, B14 64→100%. New B18: 44/50, B19: 45/50. Total: 410/450 (91%). Remaining failures: data gaps (15), wrong indicator variants (5), provider limitations (5), ambiguity (4).
